title: Sherbournia bignoniiflora
intro: Sherbournia bignoniiflora, commonly known as the African teak, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive large leaves and showy flowers. The leaves and roots of Sherbournia bignoniiflora have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Sherbournia bignoniiflora is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and ointments, to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Sherbournia bignoniiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. It is a member of the genus Sherbournia, which is a small genus of flowering plants. The plant is native to tropical Africa and is known for its showy, trumpet-shaped flowers. The classification of Sherbournia bignoniiflora is within the order Lamiales. Sherbournia bignoniiflora is a part of the larger Angiosperm phylogeny.
